encounter_id.name: encounter_id
encounter_id.description: Unique identifier for each admission/encounter. Useful for relational database mapping but should be removed during modeling to prevent data leakage, as it holds no clinical predictive value.
encounter_id.cleaning_explanation: Unique identifier. Keeping it would cause data leakage or overfitting.

patient_nbr.name: patient_nbr
patient_nbr.description: Unique identifier for each patient. Crucial for identifying readmissions, as a single patient can have multiple encounters. Must be dropped before modeling to prevent the model from memorizing specific patients.
patient_nbr.cleaning_explanation: Unique identifier. Keeping it would cause data leakage or overfitting.

race.name: race
race.description: The racial or ethnic background of the patient (Caucasian, Asian, African American, Hispanic, or Other). Important for understanding demographic disparities in diabetes care, healthcare access, and readmission rates.
race.cleaning_explanation: Categorical feature with missing values. Fill with mode or create a distinct 'Unknown' class.

gender.name: gender
gender.description: The biological sex of the patient (Male, Female, or Unknown/Invalid). Used to control for biological variance in disease progression and medication efficacy.
gender.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

age.name: age
age.description: Patient age, structured in 10-year ordinal intervals (e.g., [0-10), [10-20), up to [90-100)). Age is a primary risk factor for diabetes complications, healing rate, and readmission probability.
age.cleaning_explanation: Age brackets are converted to numeric midpoints before modeling.

weight.name: weight
weight.description: The patient's weight in pounds. While clinically vital for BMI and dosage calculations, this feature is notoriously missing >95% of its data in this dataset and is typically dropped during preprocessing.
weight.cleaning_explanation: Dropped by the canonical pipeline due to excessive missingness or unreliable socioeconomic proxy behavior.

admission_type_id.name: admission_type_id
admission_type_id.description: Integer identifier indicating the urgency or type of admission (e.g., Emergency, Urgent, Elective). Emergency admissions often correlate with higher instability and readmission risk. (Mapped from IDS_mapping.csv)
admission_type_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

discharge_disposition_id.name: discharge_disposition_id
discharge_disposition_id.description: Integer identifier indicating where the patient was discharged to (e.g., Home, Hospice, SNF). Crucial for filtering out terminal patients (Hospice/Expired) who logically cannot be readmitted. (Mapped from IDS_mapping.csv)

value_mapping.2: Discharged/transferred to another short term hospital
value_mapping.5: Discharged/transferred to another type of inpatient care institution
value_mapping.6: Discharged/transferred to home with home health service
value_mapping.8: Discharged/transferred to home under care of Home IV provider
value_mapping.9: Admitted as an inpatient to this hospital
value_mapping.10: Neonate discharged to another hospital for neonatal aftercare
value_mapping.12: Still patient or expected to return for outpatient services
value_mapping.15: Discharged/transferred within this institution to Medicare approved swing bed
value_mapping.16: Discharged/transferred/referred another institution for outpatient services
value_mapping.17: Discharged/transferred/referred to this institution for outpatient services
value_mapping.19: Expired at home. Medicaid only, hospice.
value_mapping.20: Expired in a medical facility. Medicaid only, hospice.
value_mapping.21: Expired, place unknown. Medicaid only, hospice.
value_mapping.22: Discharged/transferred to another rehab fac including rehab units of a hospital .
value_mapping.23: Discharged/transferred to a long term care hospital.
value_mapping.24: Discharged/transferred to a nursing facility certified under Medicaid but not certified under Medicare.
value_mapping.30: Discharged/transferred to another Type of Health Care Institution not Defined Elsewhere
value_mapping.27: Discharged/transferred to a federal health care facility.
value_mapping.28: Discharged/transferred/referred to a psychiatric hospital of psychiatric distinct part unit of a hospital
value_mapping.29: Discharged/transferred to a Critical Access Hospital (CAH).

discharge_disposition_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

admission_source_id.name: admission_source_id
admission_source_id.description: Integer identifier indicating where the patient came from before admission (e.g., Emergency Room, Physician Referral, Transfer). Indicates the initial severity of the patient's condition. (Mapped from IDS_mapping.csv)

value_mapping.5: Transfer from a Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF)
value_mapping.6: Transfer from another health care facility
value_mapping.18: Transfer From Another Home Health Agency
value_mapping.19: Readmission to Same Home Health Agency
value_mapping.22: Transfer from hospital inpt/same fac reslt in a sep claim

admission_source_id.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

time_in_hospital.name: time_in_hospital
time_in_hospital.description: The total duration of the hospital stay in days. Longer stays generally indicate more severe complications, intensive treatments, or slower recovery, strongly influencing readmission likelihood.
time_in_hospital.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

payer_code.name: payer_code
payer_code.description: Indicates the patient's health insurance type (e.g., Medicare, Blue Cross, Self-Pay). Can serve as a proxy for socioeconomic status, which heavily impacts post-discharge care access and medication adherence.
payer_code.cleaning_explanation: Dropped by the canonical pipeline due to excessive missingness or unreliable socioeconomic proxy behavior.

medical_specialty.name: medical_specialty
medical_specialty.description: The specialty of the admitting physician (e.g., Cardiology, Internal Medicine, Surgery). Reflects the primary physiological system being treated during the encounter.
medical_specialty.cleaning_explanation: Preserved with an explicit 'Missing' category, following Strack-style handling of specialty missingness.

num_lab_procedures.name: num_lab_procedures
num_lab_procedures.description: The total number of laboratory tests performed during the patient's stay. High numbers often correlate with diagnostic uncertainty or severe, fluctuating health conditions.
num_lab_procedures.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

num_procedures.name: num_procedures
num_procedures.description: The total number of medical procedures (excluding lab tests) performed, such as surgeries or imaging. Indicates the invasiveness and intensity of the hospital intervention.
num_procedures.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

num_medications.name: num_medications
num_medications.description: The number of distinct generic medications administered. Polypharmacy (high number of medications) is a known risk factor for adverse drug events and hospital readmissions.
num_medications.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

number_outpatient.name: number_outpatient
number_outpatient.description: Number of outpatient visits the patient had in the year prior to this encounter. High outpatient visits may indicate proactive chronic illness management or frequent follow-ups.
number_outpatient.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

number_emergency.name: number_emergency
number_emergency.description: Number of emergency room visits by the patient in the preceding year. A very strong predictor of systemic health instability, lack of primary care, and future readmissions.
number_emergency.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

number_inpatient.name: number_inpatient
number_inpatient.description: Number of times the patient was hospitalized in the preceding year. Historical hospitalization is statistically one of the strongest predictive features for future readmission.
number_inpatient.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

diag_1.name: diag_1
diag_1.description: The primary ICD-9 diagnosis code that resulted in the admission. Typically requires grouping into broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory) due to extremely high cardinality.
diag_1.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Group into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory).

diag_2.name: diag_2
diag_2.description: The secondary ICD-9 diagnosis code. Captures comorbidities that complicate the primary diagnosis. Also requires categorical grouping to be useful for machine learning models.
diag_2.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Group into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory).

diag_3.name: diag_3
diag_3.description: An additional secondary ICD-9 diagnosis code, representing further comorbid conditions. Crucial for assessing the overall complexity of the patient's health state.
diag_3.cleaning_explanation: High cardinality ICD-9 codes. Group into ~9 broader clinical categories (e.g., Circulatory, Respiratory).

number_diagnoses.name: number_diagnoses
number_diagnoses.description: The total number of diagnoses recorded in the system for this encounter. A direct proxy for patient multimorbidity (having multiple chronic conditions) and overall health complexity.
number_diagnoses.cleaning_explanation: Standard numeric feature. Ready for modeling after scaling.

max_glu_serum.name: max_glu_serum
max_glu_serum.description: The results of the maximum glucose serum test, if taken. Used to measure acute blood sugar levels. Values indicate normal, >200, or >300 mg/dL. 'None' means the test was not administered.
max_glu_serum.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

A1Cresult.name: A1Cresult
A1Cresult.description: The result of the HbA1c test, which measures average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months. Critical for assessing long-term diabetes control. Values include normal, >7%, or >8%.
A1Cresult.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

metformin.name: metformin
metformin.description: Indicates the administration status of the specific diabetic medication 'metformin' during the encounter. 'Up' means the dosage was increased, 'Down' means decreased, 'Steady' means the dosage remained the same, and 'No' indicates the drug was not prescribed. Changes in these medications often signal clinical adjustments to stabilize blood glucose.
metformin.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

repaglinide.description: Indicates the administration status of the specific diabetic medication 'repaglinide' during the encounter. 'Up' means the dosage was increased, 'Down' means decreased, 'Steady' means the dosage remained the same, and 'No' indicates the drug was not prescribed. Changes in these medications often signal clinical adjustments to stabilize blood glucose.
repaglinide.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

examide.name: examide
examide.description: Indicates the administration status of the specific diabetic medication 'examide' during the encounter. 'Up' means the dosage was increased, 'Down' means decreased, 'Steady' means the dosage remained the same, and 'No' indicates the drug was not prescribed. Changes in these medications often signal clinical adjustments to stabilize blood glucose.
examide.cleaning_explanation: Zero variance. Every row has the exact same value, offering zero predictive power.

citoglipton.description: Indicates the administration status of the specific diabetic medication 'citoglipton' during the encounter. 'Up' means the dosage was increased, 'Down' means decreased, 'Steady' means the dosage remained the same, and 'No' indicates the drug was not prescribed. metformin-pioglitazone.description: Indicates the administration status of the specific diabetic medication 'metformin-pioglitazone' during the encounter. Changes in these medications often signal clinical adjustments to stabilize blood glucose.
metformin-pioglitazone.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

change.name: change
change.description: A binary flag indicating whether the dosage or generic name of ANY diabetic medication was changed during the encounter. Medication changes often indicate uncontrolled diabetes requiring intervention.
change.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

diabetesMed.name: diabetesMed
diabetesMed.description: A binary flag indicating whether any diabetic medication was prescribed to the patient. Distinguishes between diet-controlled diabetics and medication-dependent diabetics.
diabetesMed.cleaning_explanation: Low cardinality categorical variable. One-Hot Encoding is standard.

readmitted.name: readmitted
readmitted.description: The Target Variable. Indicates if the patient was readmitted to the hospital. Classes: '<30' (within 30 days, often penalized by Medicare), '>30' (after 30 days), or 'NO' (no readmission).
readmitted.cleaning_explanation: Target variable. Convert to binary ('<30' vs 'NO' or '>30') for classification.
